email.headerregistry/Address/username.name: username
email.headerregistry/Address/username.description: The username portion of the address, with all quoting removed.

email.headerregistry/UnstructuredHeader.name: UnstructuredHeader
email.headerregistry/UnstructuredHeader.description: An “unstructured” header is the default type of header in RFC 5322. Any header that does not have a specified syntax is treated as unstructured. The classic example of an unstructured header is the Subject header. In RFC 5322, an unstructured header is a run of arbitrary text in the ASCII character set. RFC 2047, however, has an RFC 5322 compatible mechanism for encoding non-ASCII text as ASCII characters within a header value. When a value containing encoded words is passed to the constructo...
